28: // Formatter is an interface that is responsible for formatting a request/response into some format.
29: // It is recommended that you pass data through Hash prior to formatting it.
30: type Formatter interface {
31: 	// FormatRequest formats the logical.LogInput into an AuditRequestEntry.
32: 	FormatRequest(context.Context, FormatterConfig, *logical.LogInput) (*AuditRequestEntry, error)
33: 	// FormatResponse formats the logical.LogInput into an AuditResponseEntry.
34: 	FormatResponse(context.Context, FormatterConfig, *logical.LogInput) (*AuditResponseEntry, error)

37: // Writer is an interface that provides a way to write request and response audit entries.
38: // Formatters write their output to an io.Writer.

40: 	// WriteRequest writes the request entry to the writer or returns an error.
41: 	WriteRequest(io.Writer, *AuditRequestEntry) error
42: 	// WriteResponse writes the response entry to the writer or returns an error.
43: 	WriteResponse(io.Writer, *AuditResponseEntry) error
44: }

46: var (
47: 	_ Formatter = (*AuditFormatter)(nil)
48: 	_ Formatter = (*AuditFormatterWriter)(nil)
49: 	_ Writer    = (*AuditFormatterWriter)(nil)

54: 	salter Salter
55: }

57: // AuditFormatterWriter should be used to format and write out audit requests and responses.
58: type AuditFormatterWriter struct {
59: 	Formatter
60: 	Writer
61: }

63: type FormatterConfig struct {
64: 	Raw          bool
65: 	HMACAccessor bool
66: 
67: 	// Vault lacks pagination in its APIs. As a result, certain list operations can return **very** large responses.
68: 	// The user's chosen audit sinks may experience difficulty consuming audit records that swell to tens of megabytes
69: 	// of JSON. The responses of list operations are typically not very interesting, as they are mostly lists of keys,
70: 	// or, even when they include a "key_info" field, are not returning confidential information. They become even less
71: 	// interesting once HMAC-ed by the audit system.
72: 	//
73: 	// Some example Vault "list" operations that are prone to becoming very large in an active Vault installation are:
74: 	//   auth/token/accessors/
75: 	//   identity/entity/id/
76: 	//   identity/entity-alias/id/
77: 	//   pki/certs/
78: 	//
79: 	// This option exists to provide such users with the option to have response data elided from audit logs, only when
80: 	// the operation type is "list". For added safety, the elision only applies to the "keys" and "key_info" fields
81: 	// within the response data - these are conventionally the only fields present in a list response - see
82: 	// logical.ListResponse, and logical.ListResponseWithInfo. However, other fields are technically possible if a
83: 	// plugin author writes unusual code, and these will be preserved in the audit log even with this option enabled.
84: 	// The elision replaces the values of the "keys" and "key_info" fields with an integer count of the number of
85: 	// entries. This allows even the elided audit logs to still be useful for answering questions like
86: 	// "Was any data returned?" or "How many records were listed?".
87: 	ElideListResponses bool
88: 
89: 	// This should only ever be used in a testing context
90: 	OmitTime bool
91: }

99: }

127: // FormatRequest attempts to format the specified logical.LogInput into an AuditRequestEntry.
128: func (f *AuditFormatter) FormatRequest(ctx context.Context, config FormatterConfig, in *logical.LogInput) (*AuditRequestEntry, error) {
129: 	switch {
130: 	case in == nil || in.Request == nil:
131: 		return nil, errors.New("request to request-audit a nil request")
132: 	case f.salter == nil:
133: 		return nil, errors.New("salt func not configured")
134: 	}
135: 
136: 	s, err := f.salter.Salt(ctx)
137: 	if err != nil {
138: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error fetching salt: %w", err)
139: 	}
140: 
141: 	// Set these to the input values at first
142: 	auth := in.Auth
143: 	req := in.Request
144: 	var connState *tls.ConnectionState
145: 	if auth == nil {
146: 		auth = new(logical.Auth)
147: 	}
148: 
149: 	if in.Request.Connection != nil && in.Request.Connection.ConnState != nil {
150: 		connState = in.Request.Connection.ConnState
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	if !config.Raw {
154: 		auth, err = HashAuth(s, auth, config.HMACAccessor)
155: 		if err != nil {
156: 			return nil, err
157: 		}
158: 
159: 		req, err = HashRequest(s, req, config.HMACAccessor, in.NonHMACReqDataKeys)
160: 		if err != nil {
161: 			return nil, err
162: 		}
163: 	}
164: 
165: 	var errString string
166: 	if in.OuterErr != nil {
167: 		errString = in.OuterErr.Error()
168: 	}
169: 
170: 	ns, err := namespace.FromContext(ctx)
171: 	if err != nil {
172: 		return nil, err
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	reqType := in.Type
176: 	if reqType == "" {
177: 		reqType = "request"
178: 	}
179: 	reqEntry := &AuditRequestEntry{
180: 		Type:          reqType,
181: 		Error:         errString,
182: 		ForwardedFrom: req.ForwardedFrom,
183: 		Auth: &AuditAuth{
184: 			ClientToken:               auth.ClientToken,
185: 			Accessor:                  auth.Accessor,
186: 			DisplayName:               auth.DisplayName,
187: 			Policies:                  auth.Policies,
188: 			TokenPolicies:             auth.TokenPolicies,
189: 			IdentityPolicies:          auth.IdentityPolicies,
190: 			ExternalNamespacePolicies: auth.ExternalNamespacePolicies,
191: 			NoDefaultPolicy:           auth.NoDefaultPolicy,
192: 			Metadata:                  auth.Metadata,
193: 			EntityID:                  auth.EntityID,
194: 			RemainingUses:             req.ClientTokenRemainingUses,
195: 			TokenType:                 auth.TokenType.String(),
196: 			TokenTTL:                  int64(auth.TTL.Seconds()),
197: 		},
198: 
199: 		Request: &AuditRequest{
200: 			ID:                    req.ID,
201: 			ClientID:              req.ClientID,
202: 			ClientToken:           req.ClientToken,
203: 			ClientTokenAccessor:   req.ClientTokenAccessor,
204: 			Operation:             req.Operation,
205: 			MountPoint:            req.MountPoint,
206: 			MountType:             req.MountType,
207: 			MountAccessor:         req.MountAccessor,
208: 			MountRunningVersion:   req.MountRunningVersion(),
209: 			MountRunningSha256:    req.MountRunningSha256(),
210: 			MountIsExternalPlugin: req.MountIsExternalPlugin(),
211: 			MountClass:            req.MountClass(),
212: 			Namespace: &AuditNamespace{
213: 				ID:   ns.ID,
214: 				Path: ns.Path,
215: 			},
216: 			Path:                          req.Path,
217: 			Data:                          req.Data,
218: 			PolicyOverride:                req.PolicyOverride,
219: 			RemoteAddr:                    getRemoteAddr(req),
220: 			RemotePort:                    getRemotePort(req),
221: 			ReplicationCluster:            req.ReplicationCluster,
222: 			Headers:                       req.Headers,
223: 			ClientCertificateSerialNumber: getClientCertificateSerialNumber(connState),
224: 		},
225: 	}
226: 
227: 	if !auth.IssueTime.IsZero() {
228: 		reqEntry.Auth.TokenIssueTime = auth.IssueTime.Format(time.RFC3339)
229: 	}
230: 
231: 	if auth.PolicyResults != nil {
232: 		reqEntry.Auth.PolicyResults = &AuditPolicyResults{
233: 			Allowed: auth.PolicyResults.Allowed,
234: 		}
235: 
236: 		for _, p := range auth.PolicyResults.GrantingPolicies {
237: 			reqEntry.Auth.PolicyResults.GrantingPolicies = append(reqEntry.Auth.PolicyResults.GrantingPolicies, PolicyInfo{
238: 				Name:          p.Name,
239: 				NamespaceId:   p.NamespaceId,
240: 				NamespacePath: p.NamespacePath,
241: 				Type:          p.Type,
242: 			})
243: 		}
244: 	}
245: 
246: 	if req.WrapInfo != nil {
247: 		reqEntry.Request.WrapTTL = int(req.WrapInfo.TTL / time.Second)
248: 	}
249: 
250: 	if !config.OmitTime {
251: 		reqEntry.Time = time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)
252: 	}
253: 
254: 	return reqEntry, nil
255: }

257: // FormatResponse attempts to format the specified logical.LogInput into an AuditResponseEntry.
258: func (f *AuditFormatter) FormatResponse(ctx context.Context, config FormatterConfig, in *logical.LogInput) (*AuditResponseEntry, error) {
259: 	switch {
260: 	case in == nil || in.Request == nil:
261: 		return nil, errors.New("request to response-audit a nil request")
262: 	case f.salter == nil:
263: 		return nil, errors.New("salt func not configured")
264: 	}
265: 
266: 	s, err := f.salter.Salt(ctx)
267: 	if err != nil {
268: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error fetching salt: %w", err)
269: 	}
270: 
271: 	// Set these to the input values at first
272: 	auth, req, resp := in.Auth, in.Request, in.Response
273: 	if auth == nil {
274: 		auth = new(logical.Auth)
275: 	}
276: 	if resp == nil {
277: 		resp = new(logical.Response)
278: 	}
279: 	var connState *tls.ConnectionState
280: 
281: 	if in.Request.Connection != nil && in.Request.Connection.ConnState != nil {
282: 		connState = in.Request.Connection.ConnState
283: 	}
284: 
285: 	elideListResponseData := config.ElideListResponses && req.Operation == logical.ListOperation
286: 
287: 	var respData map[string]interface{}
288: 	if config.Raw {
289: 		// In the non-raw case, elision of list response data occurs inside HashResponse, to avoid redundant deep
290: 		// copies and hashing of data only to elide it later. In the raw case, we need to do it here.
291: 		if elideListResponseData && resp.Data != nil {
292: 			// Copy the data map before making changes, but we only need to go one level deep in this case
293: 			respData = make(map[string]interface{}, len(resp.Data))
294: 			for k, v := range resp.Data {
295: 				respData[k] = v
296: 			}
297: 
298: 			doElideListResponseData(respData)
299: 		} else {
300: 			respData = resp.Data
301: 		}
302: 	} else {
303: 		auth, err = HashAuth(s, auth, config.HMACAccessor)
304: 		if err != nil {
305: 			return nil, err
306: 		}
307: 
308: 		req, err = HashRequest(s, req, config.HMACAccessor, in.NonHMACReqDataKeys)
309: 		if err != nil {
310: 			return nil, err
311: 		}
312: 
313: 		resp, err = HashResponse(s, resp, config.HMACAccessor, in.NonHMACRespDataKeys, elideListResponseData)
314: 		if err != nil {
315: 			return nil, err
316: 		}
317: 
318: 		respData = resp.Data
319: 	}
320: 
321: 	var errString string
322: 	if in.OuterErr != nil {
323: 		errString = in.OuterErr.Error()
324: 	}
325: 
326: 	ns, err := namespace.FromContext(ctx)
327: 	if err != nil {
328: 		return nil, err
329: 	}
330: 
331: 	var respAuth *AuditAuth
332: 	if resp.Auth != nil {
333: 		respAuth = &AuditAuth{
334: 			ClientToken:               resp.Auth.ClientToken,
335: 			Accessor:                  resp.Auth.Accessor,
336: 			DisplayName:               resp.Auth.DisplayName,
337: 			Policies:                  resp.Auth.Policies,
338: 			TokenPolicies:             resp.Auth.TokenPolicies,
339: 			IdentityPolicies:          resp.Auth.IdentityPolicies,
340: 			ExternalNamespacePolicies: resp.Auth.ExternalNamespacePolicies,
341: 			NoDefaultPolicy:           resp.Auth.NoDefaultPolicy,
342: 			Metadata:                  resp.Auth.Metadata,
343: 			NumUses:                   resp.Auth.NumUses,
344: 			EntityID:                  resp.Auth.EntityID,
345: 			TokenType:                 resp.Auth.TokenType.String(),
346: 			TokenTTL:                  int64(resp.Auth.TTL.Seconds()),
347: 		}
348: 		if !resp.Auth.IssueTime.IsZero() {
349: 			respAuth.TokenIssueTime = resp.Auth.IssueTime.Format(time.RFC3339)
350: 		}
351: 	}
352: 
353: 	var respSecret *AuditSecret
354: 	if resp.Secret != nil {
355: 		respSecret = &AuditSecret{
356: 			LeaseID: resp.Secret.LeaseID,
357: 		}
358: 	}
359: 
360: 	var respWrapInfo *AuditResponseWrapInfo
361: 	if resp.WrapInfo != nil {
362: 		token := resp.WrapInfo.Token
363: 		if jwtToken := parseVaultTokenFromJWT(token); jwtToken != nil {
364: 			token = *jwtToken
365: 		}
366: 		respWrapInfo = &AuditResponseWrapInfo{
367: 			TTL:             int(resp.WrapInfo.TTL / time.Second),
368: 			Token:           token,
369: 			Accessor:        resp.WrapInfo.Accessor,
370: 			CreationTime:    resp.WrapInfo.CreationTime.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
371: 			CreationPath:    resp.WrapInfo.CreationPath,
372: 			WrappedAccessor: resp.WrapInfo.WrappedAccessor,
373: 		}
374: 	}
375: 
376: 	respType := in.Type
377: 	if respType == "" {
378: 		respType = "response"
379: 	}
380: 	respEntry := &AuditResponseEntry{
381: 		Type:      respType,
382: 		Error:     errString,
383: 		Forwarded: req.ForwardedFrom != "",
384: 		Auth: &AuditAuth{
385: 			ClientToken:               auth.ClientToken,
386: 			Accessor:                  auth.Accessor,
387: 			DisplayName:               auth.DisplayName,
388: 			Policies:                  auth.Policies,
389: 			TokenPolicies:             auth.TokenPolicies,
390: 			IdentityPolicies:          auth.IdentityPolicies,
391: 			ExternalNamespacePolicies: auth.ExternalNamespacePolicies,
392: 			NoDefaultPolicy:           auth.NoDefaultPolicy,
393: 			Metadata:                  auth.Metadata,
394: 			RemainingUses:             req.ClientTokenRemainingUses,
395: 			EntityID:                  auth.EntityID,
396: 			EntityCreated:             auth.EntityCreated,
397: 			TokenType:                 auth.TokenType.String(),
398: 			TokenTTL:                  int64(auth.TTL.Seconds()),
399: 		},
400: 
401: 		Request: &AuditRequest{
402: 			ID:                    req.ID,
403: 			ClientToken:           req.ClientToken,
404: 			ClientTokenAccessor:   req.ClientTokenAccessor,
405: 			ClientID:              req.ClientID,
406: 			Operation:             req.Operation,
407: 			MountPoint:            req.MountPoint,
408: 			MountType:             req.MountType,
409: 			MountAccessor:         req.MountAccessor,
410: 			MountRunningVersion:   req.MountRunningVersion(),
411: 			MountRunningSha256:    req.MountRunningSha256(),
412: 			MountIsExternalPlugin: req.MountIsExternalPlugin(),
413: 			MountClass:            req.MountClass(),
414: 			Namespace: &AuditNamespace{
415: 				ID:   ns.ID,
416: 				Path: ns.Path,
417: 			},
418: 			Path:                          req.Path,
419: 			Data:                          req.Data,
420: 			PolicyOverride:                req.PolicyOverride,
421: 			RemoteAddr:                    getRemoteAddr(req),
422: 			RemotePort:                    getRemotePort(req),
423: 			ClientCertificateSerialNumber: getClientCertificateSerialNumber(connState),
424: 			ReplicationCluster:            req.ReplicationCluster,
425: 			Headers:                       req.Headers,
426: 		},
427: 
428: 		Response: &AuditResponse{
429: 			MountPoint:            req.MountPoint,
430: 			MountType:             req.MountType,
431: 			MountAccessor:         req.MountAccessor,
432: 			MountRunningVersion:   req.MountRunningVersion(),
433: 			MountRunningSha256:    req.MountRunningSha256(),
434: 			MountIsExternalPlugin: req.MountIsExternalPlugin(),
435: 			MountClass:            req.MountClass(),
436: 			Auth:                  respAuth,
437: 			Secret:                respSecret,
438: 			Data:                  respData,
439: 			Warnings:              resp.Warnings,
440: 			Redirect:              resp.Redirect,
441: 			WrapInfo:              respWrapInfo,
442: 			Headers:               resp.Headers,
443: 		},
444: 	}
445: 
446: 	if auth.PolicyResults != nil {
447: 		respEntry.Auth.PolicyResults = &AuditPolicyResults{
448: 			Allowed: auth.PolicyResults.Allowed,
449: 		}
450: 
451: 		for _, p := range auth.PolicyResults.GrantingPolicies {
452: 			respEntry.Auth.PolicyResults.GrantingPolicies = append(respEntry.Auth.PolicyResults.GrantingPolicies, PolicyInfo{
453: 				Name:          p.Name,
454: 				NamespaceId:   p.NamespaceId,
455: 				NamespacePath: p.NamespacePath,
456: 				Type:          p.Type,
457: 			})
458: 		}
459: 	}
460: 
461: 	if !auth.IssueTime.IsZero() {
462: 		respEntry.Auth.TokenIssueTime = auth.IssueTime.Format(time.RFC3339)
463: 	}
464: 	if req.WrapInfo != nil {
465: 		respEntry.Request.WrapTTL = int(req.WrapInfo.TTL / time.Second)
466: 	}
467: 
468: 	if !config.OmitTime {
469: 		respEntry.Time = time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)
470: 	}
471: 
472: 	return respEntry, nil
473: }

475: // FormatAndWriteRequest attempts to format the specified logical.LogInput into an AuditRequestEntry,
476: // and then write the request using the specified io.Writer.
477: func (f *AuditFormatterWriter) FormatAndWriteRequest(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer, config FormatterConfig, in *logical.LogInput) error {
478: 	switch {
479: 	case in == nil || in.Request == nil:
480: 		return fmt.Errorf("request to request-audit a nil request")
481: 	case w == nil:
482: 		return fmt.Errorf("writer for audit request is nil")
483: 	case f.Formatter == nil:
484: 		return fmt.Errorf("no formatter specifed")
485: 	case f.Writer == nil:
486: 		return fmt.Errorf("no writer specified")
487: 	}
488: 
489: 	reqEntry, err := f.Formatter.FormatRequest(ctx, config, in)
490: 	if err != nil {
491: 		return err
492: 	}
493: 
494: 	return f.Writer.WriteRequest(w, reqEntry)
495: }
496: 
497: // FormatAndWriteResponse attempts to format the specified logical.LogInput into an AuditResponseEntry,
498: // and then write the response using the specified io.Writer.
499: func (f *AuditFormatterWriter) FormatAndWriteResponse(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer, config FormatterConfig, in *logical.LogInput) error {
500: 	switch {
501: 	case in == nil || in.Request == nil:
502: 		return errors.New("request to response-audit a nil request")
503: 	case w == nil:
504: 		return errors.New("writer for audit request is nil")
505: 	case f.Formatter == nil:
506: 		return errors.New("no formatter specified")
507: 	case f.Writer == nil:
508: 		return errors.New("no writer specified")
509: 	}
510: 
511: 	respEntry, err := f.FormatResponse(ctx, config, in)
512: 	if err != nil {
513: 		return err
514: 	}
515: 
516: 	return f.Writer.WriteResponse(w, respEntry)
517: }

19: // Option is how Options are passed as arguments.
20: type Option func(*options) error
21: 
22: // options are used to represent configuration for an Event.
23: type options struct {
24: 	withID          string
25: 	withNow         time.Time
26: 	withSubtype     auditSubtype

33: 	withMaxDuration time.Duration
34: }
35: 
36: // getDefaultOptions returns options with their default values.
37: func getDefaultOptions() options {
38: 	return options{
39: 		withNow:         time.Now(),
40: 		withFacility:    "AUTH",
41: 		withTag:         "vault",
42: 		withSocketType:  "tcp",
43: 		withMaxDuration: 2 * time.Second,
44: 	}
45: }
46: 
47: // getOpts applies all the supplied Option and returns configured options.
48: // Each Option is applied in the order it appears in the argument list, so it is
49: // possible to supply the same Option numerous times and the 'last write wins'.
50: func getOpts(opt ...Option) (options, error) {
51: 	opts := getDefaultOptions()
52: 	for _, o := range opt {
53: 		if o == nil {
54: 			continue
55: 		}
56: 		if err := o(&opts); err != nil {
57: 			return options{}, err
58: 		}
59: 	}
60: 	return opts, nil
61: }
62: 
63: // NewID is a bit of a modified NewID has been done to stop a circular
64: // dependency with the errors package that is caused by importing
65: // boundary/internal/db
66: func NewID(prefix string) (string, error) {
67: 	const op = "event.NewID"
68: 	if prefix == "" {
69: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("%s: missing prefix: %w", op, ErrInvalidParameter)
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	id, err := uuid.GenerateUUID()
73: 	if err != nil {
74: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("%s: unable to generate ID: %w", op, err)
75: 	}
76: 
77: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s", prefix, id), nil
78: }
79: 
80: // WithID provides an optional ID.
81: func WithID(id string) Option {
82: 	return func(o *options) error {
83: 		var err error
84: 
85: 		id := strings.TrimSpace(id)
86: 		switch {
87: 		case id == "":
88: 			err = errors.New("id cannot be empty")

129: 	}
130: }
131:

134: 	return func(o *options) error {
135: 		f := strings.TrimSpace(format)

137: 			return errors.New("format cannot be empty")

144: 		}
145:

148: 	}
149: }

154: func WithFileMode(mode string) Option {

177: 	}

func fakeJSONAuditEvent(tb testing.TB, subtype auditSubtype, input *logical.LogInput) *eventlogger.Event {
	tb.Helper()

	date := time.Date(2023, time.July, 11, 15, 49, 10, 0o0, time.Local)

	auditEvent, err := newAudit(
		WithID("123"),
		WithSubtype(string(subtype)),
		WithFormat(string(AuditFormatJSON)),
		WithNow(date),
	)
	require.NoError(tb, err)
	require.NotNil(tb, auditEvent)
	require.Equal(tb, "123", auditEvent.ID)
	require.Equal(tb, "v0.1", auditEvent.Version)
	require.Equal(tb, AuditFormatJSON, auditEvent.RequiredFormat)
	require.Equal(tb, subtype, auditEvent.Subtype)
	require.Equal(tb, date, auditEvent.Timestamp)

	auditEvent.Data = input
